items.title:
Food security looks very different from wealth.

Only one country - Guyana - can produce all seven essential food groups domestically.

Most others, including large economies like the U.S. and China, rely on imports in at least one category.

That highlights a structural reality.

Modern food systems are built on interdependence, not self-sufficiency.

Trade enables variety and efficiency.
But it also introduces dependency.

The takeaway isn’t that every country should aim for full independence.

It’s that resilience matters.

Diversified supply chains, local capacity, and strategic buffers become critical when disruptions occur.

Because food isn’t just another commodity.

It’s foundational.

#FoodSecurity #SupplyChain #GlobalEconomy #Resilience

Source 🙏🏻@VisualCap

items.title:
Maybe robots will not replace us.

At least not as quickly as the headlines suggest.

Watch enough real-world footage, and you see it:

Robots slipping.
Missing objects.
Failing in simple, unpredictable situations.

Not because the technology is bad.

But because the real world is messy.

Uneven surfaces.
Changing conditions.
Ambiguous inputs.

Things humans handle without thinking.

We tend to judge progress by demos.

But deployment is different.

Reliability, adaptability, and edge cases decide whether systems actually work outside controlled environments.

So maybe the future isn’t robots replacing humans.

It’s robots handling structured tasks - while humans remain essential where judgment, improvisation, and context matter.

The gap is closing.

But it’s not gone.

#Robotics #AI #Automation #FutureOfWork

These are well-defined detection patterns (regex for secrets, schema checks, URI validation) that would meaningfully improve security posture when connecting to MCP servers.
